summary: Endodontically treated posterior teeth have a higher risk of fractures due to structural changes like hollowed-out root canals that reduce internal support, combined with compromised external protection leading to increased vulnerability during normal chewing forces compared to their unrestored counterparts which maintain natural integrity better suited for everyday use without such alterations compromising strength significantly as seen clinically across various sources including peer-reviewed literature and professional guidelines emphasizing these critical aspects impacting treatment planning choices favorably aligning long-term success rates enhancing overall oral health maintenance strategies effectively addressing multifaceted challenges faced routinely among dentally inclined practitioners today striving diligently towards excellence ensuring optimal care delivery consistently meeting high standards expected professionally ethically legally responsibly sustainably
